0.text: Oskar v (1) Olindo (2) Onita (3) Oron [2024] DIFC CA 009 OCTOBER 30, 2024 COURT OF APPEAL - JUDGMENTS

1.text: IN THE DUBAI INTERNATIONAL FINANCIAL CENTRE COURTS

2.text: In the name of His Highness Sheikh Mohammad Bin Rashid Al Maktoum, Ruler of Dubai

4.text: BEFORE CHIEF JUSTICE WAYNE MARTIN, H.E. DEPUTY CHIEF JUSTICE ALI AL MADHANI AND JUSTICE ANDREW MORAN

Counsel :Mr Rupert Reed KC and Mr Max Marenbon instructed by Eversheds Sutherland International LLP for the Appellant/Claiman...

15.text: JUDGMENT OF THE COURT OF APPEAL

16.text: UPON the hearing of the Appellant’s appeal against the Order of H.E. Justice Nassir Al Nasser (the “Judge”) of 17 May 2024 (the “Order”), granting the relief sought by the Respondents in their Urgent ...

17.text: AND UPON reviewing the Orders of the Assistant Registrar Hayley Norton dated 11 January and 25 March 2024, (the “First Part 50 Order” and the “Second Part 50 Order” respectively).

18.text: AND UPON reviewing the documents and materials on the court fi le in the Application and in the Appeal, and the Bundle for the hearing of the Appeal (the “Bundle”), the Grounds of Appeal and the Skele...

19.text: AND UPON hearing Counsel for the Appellant and Counsel for the Respondents at the Appeal hearing held before Chief Justice Wayne Martin, H.E. Deputy Chief Justice Ali Al Madhani and Justice Andrew Mor...

20.text: IT IS HEREBY ORDERED AND DIRECTED THAT:

21.text:
1. The Appeal is allowed, to the following extent.   
2. Save and insofar as the Judge ordered that the Second Part 50 Order should continue to apply against Orvell and be stayed its effect against th...

23.text: “YOU, (i) ORVELL , (ii) ONITA , MUST OBEY THIS ORDER. IF YOU DO NOT, YOU MAY BE FINED OR REFERRED TO THE ATTORNEY GENERAL OF DUBAI FOR CONTEMPT OF COURT.

24.text:
1. An examination hearing to be held before H.E. Justice Maha Al Mheiri at 11am on Wednesday   
20 November 2024 (the “Second Examination Hearing”).

25.text: 2. Pursuant to RDC 50.2(2), the Judgment Debtor’s Representatives must attend the virtual Second Examination Hearing.

26.text: 3. An electronic link to the virtual hearing will be circulated by email to the parties approximately 7 days before the Second Examination Hearing.

27.text: 4. At the Second Examination Hearing, the Judgment Debtor’s Representatives shall answer on oath such questions as the Court may require and as may be asked by the Court or the Judgment Creditor and/o...

28.text: 5. Pursuant to RDC 50.10 and 50.5(6), the Judgment Debtor’s Representatives shall provide information about the Judgment Debtor’s assets, means and any other information which is required to enforce t...

29.text: 6. Pursuant to RDC 50.5(6), the Judgment Debtor’s Representatives shall produce all documents in the Judgment Debtor’s and Judgment Debtor’s Representatives’ control that relate to the Judgment Debtor...

30.text: 7. The Judgment Debtor’s Representatives shall produce to the Court and to the Judgment Creditors the documents set out in Schedule A to this Order, by email on enforcement@difccourts.ae by no later t...

31.text: 8. The Judgment Debtor must contact the Court by no later than 13 November 2024 by emailing enforcement@difccourts.ae to make their email addresses and contact details known to the Registry.

32.text: 9. Costs shall be determined at the Second Examination Hearing.

33.text:
4. That the Respondents shall pay the Appellant’s costs of the Appeal assessed in the amount USD   
60,541.25.

43.text: 6. On 1 April 2024, the Respondents made the Application seeking relief by way of an order sought that the Judge set out at paragraph 1 of his reasons thus:

44.text: (a) A declaration from the Courts that it has no jurisdiction to hear the claim or grant relief/s or order/s sought by the Judgment Creditor against the Applicants;

45.text: (b) Alternatively, the Court declares that it should not exercise any jurisdiction which it may have to hear the claim or grant relief/s or order/s sought by the Judgment Creditor against the Applican...

49.text: (f) The costs occasioned by this Application, are to be paid by the Judgment Creditor to the Applicants, to be assessed if not agreed.

74.text: “A judgment creditor may apply for an order requiring:
